content:
Absolutely! The question "Do you only work hard when your boss is around?" is often used in job interviews or workplace evaluations to assess your work ethic, integrity, and level of self-motivation.

**Why is "No, I always work hard" the best answer?**

1. **Shows Integrity:** Saying that you always work hard—even when unsupervised—demonstrates that you have strong personal values and can be trusted to do your job well, regardless of who is watching.

2. **Demonstrates Self-Motivation:** Employers want employees who are self-driven and take initiative. If you only work hard when your boss is around, it suggests you need external motivation and may not be as reliable.

3. **Reflects Professionalism:** A good work ethic means doing your best at all times, not just when you're being observed. This is a valuable quality in any employee.

4. **Builds Trust:** Employers need to trust their staff to maintain productivity and quality even when not directly supervised. Consistent hard work, regardless of supervision, is key to earning that trust.

**How to expand on your answer:**
If asked this question in an interview, don't just say "No, I always work hard." Give a brief example, such as:
> "No, I always work hard because I take pride in my work and understand that my performance reflects on me, whether or not anyone is watching. For example, in my last role, I often met deadlines ahead of schedule even when working independently."

**Summary:**  
Saying you always work hard, regardless of supervision, highlights your integrity, self-motivation, and professionalism—all traits employers highly value.
